content.full_text:
January 22, 1992


Legal Division
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

Re:  Request for Informal Advice

Greetings:

     Antioch City Council Member Barbara Price has
authorized and requested me to seek the FPPC's opinion on the
following matter.  A copy of your response can be mailed to
Mrs Price at 3243 Ashley Way, Antioch, CA  94509, although I
would like either the original or a copy as well.

     Fire protection services are provided to the City of
Antioch by the Riverview Fire Protection District, a district
organized pursuant to the Fire Protection District Law of
1961, Health & Safety Code §§13801 - 13999.  The District
provides service to the incorporated cities of Antioch and
Pittsburg, and to the unincorporated community of West
Pittsburg.  Mrs. Price's husband is employed by the District
as a firefighter.

     The District is governed by the Contra Costa County
Board of Supervisors, although there is a local appointed fire
commission which makes recommendations to the Board.

     A number of ideas about the District's future
organization are being discussed by the Antioch City Council.
These include proposals to remove the Board of Supervisors as
the governing body, and to replace it with a locally-elected
fire board.  Another proposal is to dissolve the District,
thereby creating a new fire department within the City of
Antioch.

     Mrs. Price's husband is also the president of the
District's firefighters' union, and receives an annual salary
or stipend for this office in excess of $250.00.  This salary
or stipend for being president of the union is paid by the
firefighters' membership dues.

Legal Division
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
January 22, 1992
Page two


It appears relatively clear to the undersigned that
no conflict of interest exists by the mere fact of Mr. Price's
employment with the Fire District by virtue of Government Code
§82030(b)(2), which excludes from the definition of "income"
any salary received from a local government agency. However,
the question posed is whether a conflict may be created by his
receipt of salary or stipend from the firefighters union by
virtue of him holding the position of president.

A question has been raised about this issue because
the reorganization of the Fire District could be perceived as
having an effect on how future labor relations of the Fire
District (or new City Department) would be conducted. For
example, instead of the County Board of Supervisors making
decisions on labor relations issues, a locally-elected fire
district board, or the City Council itself, could determine
those issues.

There also appears to be an issue of whether the
financial effect, if any, for the officeholder would be
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally.

We will look forward to your response to this
inquiry, and thank you in advance for your consideration of
these issues.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

WILLIAM R. GALSTAN
City Attorney


WRG/sh
cc: Council Member Barbara Price
Council Member Elizabeth Rimbaault
